title: Indian Shuttlers Should Learn to Think on Their Feet, Win Without a Coach Around
indian_angle: This article discusses the importance of Indian badminton players developing independent thinking to succeed without constant coaching support.
summary: The piece advocates for Indian shuttlers to cultivate self-reliance and tactical acumen on court, enabling them to win matches even when a coach is not present.

title: Badminton's 3x15 Scoring for All Domestic Tournaments, to Debut at Ernakulam
indian_angle: This news highlights a significant change in the scoring system for all domestic badminton tournaments in India, starting in Ernakulam.
summary: A new 3x15 scoring format will be implemented across all domestic badminton tournaments, with its inaugural use scheduled for an event in Ernakulam.

title: Kidambi Srikanth Falters at Last Step in US Open Final
indian_angle: Indian star Kidambi Srikanth reached the final of the US Open, showcasing his continued prowess on the international stage.
summary: Kidambi Srikanth was unable to clinch the title at the US Open, losing in the final match after a strong tournament run.

title: Kidambi Srikanth in US Open Final: When He Overturned a Point Given to Him
indian_angle: This feature highlights a remarkable moment of sportsmanship by Kidambi Srikanth during his US Open final appearance.
summary: During his US Open final, Kidambi Srikanth demonstrated exceptional sportsmanship by correcting a point that had been wrongly awarded to him.

title: Cristiano Ronaldo Fan, Shuttler Rounak Chouhan Defeats World No 6 at US Open
indian_angle: Indian shuttler Rounak Chouhan achieved a significant upset by defeating the World No. 6 at the US Open.
summary: Rounak Chouhan, an Indian shuttler and fan of Cristiano Ronaldo, caused a major upset by beating the World No. 6 at the US Open.

title: Thomas Cup Squad Reassembles for Asian Games: Treesa-Gayatri Return
indian_angle: The Indian Thomas Cup squad is regrouping for the Asian Games, with the notable return of the Treesa-Gayatri pair.
summary: The Indian badminton team, including the returning Treesa Jolly and Gayatri Gopichand, is reassembling to prepare for the upcoming Asian Games.
